add_webapp_dialog_body: Choose whether to create a new WebApp manually or import a .wapp export.

settings_export_hint: Export all available WebApps into one importable file.
settings_about_nav_hint: Version, scope, managed profiles, imports, exports and third-party integrations.
settings_about_intro: WebApp Manager creates Linux web app launchers with dedicated browser profiles and central settings for each app.
settings_about_app_body_1: The app focuses on creating, editing, importing and exporting WebApps while keeping browser-specific settings in one place.
settings_about_app_body_2: Each WebApp can store its own engine, URL, runtime behavior, appearance options and optional custom assets.
settings_about_profiles_body_1: For supported browsers, WebApp Manager creates dedicated managed profile directories so each WebApp can stay separated from your normal browser profile.
settings_about_profiles_body_2: Profile settings such as cookies-on-exit, user agent overrides, startup behavior and browser options are applied inside those managed profiles.
settings_about_integrations_body_1: Firefox integrations such as uBlock Origin and the project-maintained Swipe add-on can be enabled for managed WebApp profiles.
settings_about_integrations_body_2: Swipe is maintained as an add-on of this project. uBlock Origin remains an external third-party add-on and stays subject to its upstream license and distribution terms.
settings_about_repositories_body_1: The project currently references external upstream repositories for integrated browser components and third-party add-ons. Those repositories remain owned and maintained by their respective authors.
settings_about_repositories_body_3: Swipe Gestures (Own Add-on): included in this project.
settings_about_exports_body_1: Single WebApps can be exported as .wapp files and later imported again. The settings page can also export all available WebApps into one importable bundle.
settings_about_exports_body_2: Shared CSS and JavaScript assets are managed centrally and can then be linked to individual WebApps when needed.
settings_security_privacy_nav_hint: How data is stored locally, what the app changes, and where add-ons or custom code increase trust requirements.
settings_security_privacy_intro: The app is designed to work on managed WebApp profiles, but some features intentionally increase browser control and should only be used with trusted inputs.
settings_security_privacy_profiles_body_: Profile changes are intended for managed Firefox, Chrome and Chromium WebApp profiles. The code explicitly refuses to apply profile settings to non-managed browser profiles.
This reduces the risk of changing your everyday browser setup, but each managed WebApp profile can still store its own cookies, sessions, cache and site data.
settings_security_privacy_storage_body_1: WebApp titles, descriptions, options, exported launchers, imported assets and managed browser profile data are stored locally on your system.
settings_security_privacy_storage_body_2: If a WebApp uses a persistent profile, websites opened through that WebApp may keep cookies, local storage, login sessions and other browser data until you clear or delete the profile.
settings_security_privacy_assets_body_1: Linked or inline CSS and JavaScript can change how a site looks and behaves. Only use assets you trust, especially when JavaScript is involved.
settings_security_privacy_assets_body_2: Custom JavaScript is particularly sensitive because it can interact with page content inside the managed WebApp context and may depend on browser/runtime support.
settings_security_privacy_addons_body_1: uBlock Origin is an external Firefox add-on. The Swipe add-on is maintained in this project and is only installed for managed WebApp profiles.
settings_security_privacy_addons_body_2: Developer Mode is the highest-risk path because it allows a local unsigned Swipe add-on for managed Firefox profiles. Keep it disabled unless you are actively developing or testing that add-on.
settings_security_privacy_recommendation: Use separate WebApps only for sites you trust, review imported profiles before reusing them, and delete unused managed profiles when they are no longer needed.
Only import custom assets or enable browser add-ons when you understand their source and purpose. For sensitive accounts, prefer the smallest possible feature set.
